sect1.title: Software Requirements
sect1.para: To compile and run DDCcontrol, you need to have these packages installed:

In addition to the library packages, you also need the development packages, which are not usually installed. For example, on Debian, their names are libxml2-dev and libgtk-3-dev. Other distributions probably have similar package names. On Fedora Core 5, you will also need gnome-panel-devel package.

note.para: You don't need gtk+ if you don't plan to use the Gnome GUI.

sect1.title: Download the sources
sect1.para: Clone the source repositories from GitHub:
You should now have two directories, ddccontrol and ddccontrol-db.
Alternatively, download the latest source tarballs from the releases page and unpack them with:
sect1.screen: # tar xvfz ddccontrol-X.Y.tar.gz # tar xvfz ddccontrol-db-YYYYMMDD.tar.gz

sect1.title: Compile and install
sect1.para: If you are building from a Git checkout, you must first generate the configure script using the autotools build system:
sect1.screen: # ./configure # make # su root Password: <root password> # make install
sect1.para: You need to do this in ddccontrol and ddccontrol-db directories.
If you set the --prefix parameter when configuring one package, you must set it to the same value when configuring the other.

sect1.title: Configure /dev/i2c-* devices
sect1.para: DDCcontrol only uses userspace I2C access through /dev/i2c-* devices. Legacy direct PCI memory access and AMD ADL support have been removed, and USB-connected DDC/CI displays are not supported yet.
The kernel graphics driver must expose the monitor DDC bus as an I2C adapter, and the i2c-dev module must be loaded. To load it manually, type as root:

If you want to automatically load this module when Linux starts, see your distribution's documentation. DDCcontrol also installs a modules-load.d configuration file for this purpose on systems that use systemd.

To allow standard (i.e. non-root) users to use DDCcontrol, grant read and write access to the relevant /dev/i2c-* devices. Many distributions do this with an i2c group, for example by adding your user to that group. For quick local testing, this can also be done by typing as root:

sect2.title: Kernel configuration options
sect2.para: If i2c-dev or the relevant graphics driver support is not available with your kernel, you can build your own kernel with the following options turned on (we recommend building them as modules):

itemizedlist.listitem: Devices drivers I2C support I2C device interface .
Devices drivers Graphics support Direct Rendering Manager .
and a specific driver for your graphics card.

sect1.title: Installation on Debian (and derivatives)
sect1.para: The ddccontrol and ddccontrol-db packages are available in Debian (and derivatives). You simply need to install the ddccontrol package and all the proper dependencies will be installed along with it. For example:
